input_text: Dasar Bumiputera ni memang ada pro dan kontra dia. Dari segi sejarah, ia membantu mengurangkan jurang ekonomi antara kaum, tapi kita kena bincang sama ada ia masih relevan dalam bentuk asal atau perlu diubah suai untuk zaman sekarang.
reason: Analytical discussion of Bumiputera policy acknowledging both pros and cons without stereotyping any group.

input_text: Saya rasa perdebatan tentang sekolah vernakular ni patut difokuskan kepada kualiti pendidikan, bukan soal kaum. Apa yang penting ialah setiap anak Malaysia dapat pendidikan terbaik, sama ada di SK, SJKC, atau SJKT.
reason: Vernacular school debate framed on educational quality grounds, explicitly avoiding racial framing.

input_text: Perbincangan mengenai hudud perlu dilakukan dengan penuh hormat. Ada pelbagai pandangan dalam kalangan umat Islam sendiri, dan kita boleh berbeza pendapat tanpa perlu bermusuhan.
reason: Respectful framing of hudud law discussion acknowledging diverse Muslim perspectives without hostility.

input_text: Isu hak tanah Orang Asli ni serius dan perlu diberi perhatian. Mereka mempunyai hak adat yang sepatutnya diiktiraf oleh undang-undang, dan kita sebagai rakyat Malaysia perlu menyokong perjuangan mereka.
reason: Advocacy for Orang Asli land rights framed as legal and human rights issue. Supportive and constructive.

input_text: Pemansuhan hukuman mati adalah satu langkah progresif. Banyak negara maju sudah mansuhkan, dan Malaysia boleh pertimbangkan alternatif seperti penjara seumur hidup untuk jenayah berat.
reason: Death penalty abolition advocacy citing international precedent and suggesting alternatives. Policy discussion.

input_text: Akta Hasutan ni dah lama perlu diperbaharui. Dalam demokrasi yang sihat, rakyat patut boleh mengkritik dasar kerajaan tanpa takut didakwa di bawah undang-undang lapuk.
reason: Sedition Act reform argument framed as democratic principle. Critique of law, not incitement.

input_text: SOSMA ni menggantikan ISA, tapi ramai pengkritik kata ia masih ada masalah yang sama, iaitu penahanan tanpa bicara. Kita perlu pastikan undang-undang keselamatan seimbang dengan hak asasi manusia.
reason: SOSMA/ISA criticism focused on detention without trial, balanced with security considerations. Policy critique.

input_text: Malaysia perlu naikkan ranking kebebasan akhbar kita. Wartawan patut boleh membuat liputan tanpa gangguan, kerana akhbar bebas adalah tonggak demokrasi yang sihat.
reason: Press freedom advocacy citing democratic principles. Constructive call for improvement.

input_text: Perkahwinan kanak-kanak mesti dihentikan sepenuhnya. Tak kira apa alasan pun, kanak-kanak bawah 18 tahun belum matang untuk berkahwin dan ini boleh memudaratkan masa depan mereka.
reason: Anti-child marriage advocacy focused on child welfare. Strong position but not hateful toward any group.

input_text: Kita perlu ada dasar yang lebih baik untuk pelarian di Malaysia. Mereka ni manusia juga, lari dari perang dan keganasan. UNHCR kata Malaysia ada lebih 180,000 pelarian berdaftar, dan mereka perlu perlindungan.
reason: Compassionate discussion of refugee policy citing UNHCR data. Humanizing language without attacking any group.

input_text: Isu penukaran agama di Malaysia memang sensitif, tapi kita boleh bincang secara fakta. Undang-undang di setiap negeri berbeza, dan ini kadang menimbulkan komplikasi dalam kes mahkamah.
reason: Factual discussion of religious conversion rights noting legal variations across states. Neutral and informative.

input_text: Saya tak setuju dengan yang kata sekolah vernakular memecahbelahkan rakyat. Kajian menunjukkan pelajar sekolah vernakular tetap berjaya dalam kehidupan dan bercampur dengan semua kaum selepas tamat sekolah.
reason: Defense of vernacular schools using research-based argument. Disagrees respectfully without attacking opponents.

input_text: Dasar tindakan afirmatif macam NEP dan DEB tu ada tujuan baik pada mulanya, iaitu untuk mengurangkan kemiskinan dan ketidakseimbangan ekonomi. Tapi kita kena evaluate keberkesanannya secara jujur.
reason: Analytical evaluation of affirmative action policies acknowledging original intent while calling for honest assessment.

input_text: Kalau kita nak bincang hak LGBTQ dalam konteks undang-undang Malaysia, kita kena faham dulu apa yang Perlembagaan kata dan apa yang undang-undang negeri peruntukkan. Ini perbincangan undang-undang, bukan soal moral.
reason: LGBTQ rights discussion framed strictly as legal analysis, explicitly separating law from moral judgment. Factual approach.

input_text: Kita kena dengar suara masyarakat Orang Asli. Projek pembangunan yang merampas tanah mereka tanpa pampasan yang adil adalah tidak beretika dan melanggar hak mereka.
reason: Orang Asli land rights advocacy critiquing unfair development practices. Ethical argument without targeting any group.

input_text: Sebelum nak kata dasar Bumiputera tak adil, kena faham konteks sejarah 1969 dan kenapa NEP diwujudkan. Tapi pada masa yang sama, kita boleh bincang macam mana nak perbaiki pelaksanaannya.
reason: Contextualized Bumiputera policy discussion providing historical background while open to reform discussion.

input_text: Saya sokong pemansuhan hukuman mati mandatori, bukan kerana nak bagi peluang kepada penjenayah, tapi kerana sistem keadilan kita tak sempurna dan kesilapan kehakiman boleh berlaku.
reason: Death penalty abolition argument based on judicial fallibility. Nuanced position with clear reasoning.

input_text: Kenapa Malaysia masih rendah dalam indeks kebebasan akhbar? Kita ada banyak portal berita bebas, tapi undang-undang seperti Akta Mesin Cetak masih mengekang kebebasan media.
reason: Press freedom analysis identifying specific legislation as constraint. Questioning and analytical tone.

input_text: Anak kecil bawah 16 tahun yang dikahwinkan akan hilang peluang pendidikan dan masa depan mereka. Ini bukan isu agama, ini isu perlindungan kanak-kanak yang universal.
reason: Anti-child marriage advocacy focusing on educational and developmental impact. Universal child rights framing.

input_text: Pelarian Rohingya di Malaysia perlukan akses kepada pendidikan dan penjagaan kesihatan asas. Kita boleh bincang cabaran logistik tanpa perlu menjadikan mereka kambing hitam masalah negara.
reason: Compassionate refugee discussion advocating for basic rights while acknowledging logistical challenges.

input_text: Ada yang kata Akta Hasutan patut dimansuhkan terus, ada yang kata pinda je. Kedua-dua pandangan ada merit, tapi yang penting kita pastikan rakyat tak ditangkap semata-mata kerana bersuara.
reason: Balanced Sedition Act reform discussion presenting both abolition and amendment views. Free speech advocacy.

input_text: Perbincangan tentang hudud di Kelantan dan Terengganu perlu dilihat dari sudut perlembagaan, bukan emosi. Apa bidang kuasa mahkamah syariah dan apa had yang ditetapkan Perlembagaan Persekutuan?
reason: Hudud discussion framed as constitutional analysis rather than emotional debate. Legal and factual approach.

input_text: Kalau kita betul-betul nak kurangkan jurang ekonomi antara kaum, mungkin kita patut fokus kepada dasar berasaskan keperluan, bukan berasaskan kaum. Ini bukan bermakna dasar Bumiputera gagal, tapi ia boleh ditambah baik.
reason: Needs-based policy suggestion as improvement to Bumiputera policy. Constructive reform proposal without dismissing existing policy.

input_text: Saya rasa undang-undang penukaran agama di Malaysia perlu lebih jelas dan konsisten antara negeri-negeri. Ini bukan soal nak senangkan penukaran, tapi soal kejelasan undang-undang.
reason: Religious conversion law discussion focused on legal clarity and consistency. Neutral policy suggestion.

input_text: Saya harap generasi muda akan lebih terbuka untuk bincang isu-isu sensitif macam hak Orang Asli, hukuman mati, dan kebebasan akhbar tanpa rasa takut. Itulah tanda masyarakat yang matang.
reason: Meta-commentary encouraging open discussion of sensitive topics as sign of societal maturity. Promotes dialogue.

input_text: ISA dulu dan SOSMA sekarang, kedua-duanya ada tujuan keselamatan negara. Tapi kita perlu mekanisme semak dan imbang yang kuat supaya ia tak disalahgunakan untuk tujuan politik.
reason: ISA/SOSMA discussion acknowledging security purpose while advocating for checks and balances. Balanced critique.

input_text: Sekolah kebangsaan, SJKC, SJKT semuanya ada kelebihan masing-masing. Yang penting kurikulum kena pastikan semua pelajar fasih Bahasa Malaysia dan boleh berkomunikasi antara satu sama lain.
reason: Inclusive vernacular school position emphasizing national language proficiency as unifying factor across school types.

input_text: Dari perspektif hak asasi manusia, setiap individu termasuk komuniti LGBT di Malaysia dilindungi oleh Perkara 5 dan 8 Perlembagaan. Ini fakta undang-undang yang boleh kita bincang secara objektif.
reason: LGBTQ rights discussion citing specific constitutional articles. Strictly legal and factual framing.

input_text: Kerajaan perlu lebih telus dalam mengendalikan isu pelarian. Berapa kos sebenarnya, apa rancangan jangka panjang, dan bagaimana kita boleh bekerja dengan UNHCR dengan lebih berkesan?
reason: Refugee policy discussion calling for government transparency and international cooperation. Constructive questions.

input_text: Perkahwinan bawah umur bukan budaya kita. Ini satu masalah yang perlu ditangani melalui pendidikan dan undang-undang yang lebih ketat, demi melindungi masa depan anak-anak Malaysia.
reason: Anti-child marriage advocacy through education and legislation. Clear stance on child protection.

input_text: Pemimpin Orang Asli patut diberi lebih banyak ruang dalam perbincangan dasar yang melibatkan tanah adat mereka. Nothing about us without us, kan?
reason: Orang Asli representation advocacy using participatory governance principle. Empowering indigenous voices.

input_text: Kita boleh belajar dari negara lain macam mana mereka handle isu dasar tindakan afirmatif. India ada reservation system, Amerika ada affirmative action. Setiap negara ada pendekatan berbeza.
reason: Comparative policy analysis of affirmative action across countries. Academic and informative approach.

input_text: Saya rasa perbahasan tentang hudud di Malaysia sebenarnya mencerminkan perbahasan yang lebih besar tentang hubungan antara undang-undang sivil dan syariah dalam sistem dual kita.
reason: Hudud discussion framed within broader constitutional framework of civil-shariah duality. Academic analysis.

input_text: Hak kebebasan bersuara dalam Perkara 10 Perlembagaan ada hadnya, tapi had tersebut sepatutnya ditafsir secara sempit, bukan digunakan untuk menyekat sebarang kritikan terhadap kerajaan.
reason: Constitutional analysis of free speech limits arguing for narrow interpretation. Legal scholarship tone.

input_text: Dasar Bumiputera yang berasaskan merit dan keperluan sebenar mungkin lebih berkesan daripada dasar yang hanya berasaskan identiti kaum. Ini pandangan banyak ahli ekonomi Malaysia.
reason: Merit-based reform proposal for Bumiputera policy citing economist views. Constructive policy suggestion.

input_text: The Bumiputera policy has been a cornerstone of Malaysian economic planning since the NEP era. Whether one supports or critiques it, we should acknowledge the historical context that led to its creation while discussing reforms.
reason: Balanced English-language discussion of Bumiputera policy encouraging contextual understanding alongside reform discussion.

input_text: Malaysia's vernacular school system is actually quite unique in Southeast Asia. Few countries maintain publicly funded schools in multiple languages. The debate should center on educational outcomes, not ethnic divisions.
reason: Vernacular school debate reframed around educational outcomes with comparative regional context. Analytical and fair.

input_text: The hudud debate in Malaysia involves complex constitutional questions about state vs federal jurisdiction. It's worth studying the legal arguments on both sides rather than dismissing either position outright.
reason: Hudud discussion approached as constitutional law question encouraging study of multiple perspectives.

input_text: Religious conversion cases in Malaysia often highlight tensions between civil and syariah courts. The Lina Joy case, for instance, raised important questions about constitutional rights and judicial jurisdiction.
reason: Factual discussion of conversion rights citing a landmark case. Legal and analytical approach.

input_text: Orang Asli communities have been fighting for their customary land rights for decades. The courts have recognized native customary rights in several landmark decisions, but enforcement remains a challenge.
reason: Orang Asli land rights advocacy citing court decisions and identifying enforcement gaps. Factual and supportive.

input_text: Malaysia's decision to abolish the mandatory death penalty was a significant step forward. The question now is how to implement alternative sentencing that still serves justice while respecting human dignity.
reason: Death penalty abolition discussion focused on implementation of alternatives. Progressive but pragmatic.

input_text: The discussion around LGBTQ rights in Malaysia needs to be grounded in legal reality. While cultural and religious perspectives matter, we should also understand what international human rights frameworks say.
reason: LGBTQ rights discussion balancing local cultural context with international human rights frameworks. Factual and measured.

input_text: The Sedition Act has been used against politicians, academics, and journalists alike. Many legal scholars argue it's incompatible with a modern democracy and should be replaced with more targeted legislation.
reason: Sedition Act reform argument citing legal scholarship and calling for modernized legislation. Policy critique.

input_text: SOSMA was introduced as a counter-terrorism measure, but its provisions allowing extended detention without trial raise serious concerns. We can support national security while also demanding proper safeguards.
reason: SOSMA critique balancing security needs with human rights safeguards. Nuanced and constructive.

input_text: Malaysia's press freedom ranking has fluctuated over the years. While we've seen some improvements, issues like media ownership concentration and licensing requirements continue to limit editorial independence.
reason: Press freedom analysis identifying specific structural issues. Factual and analytical.

input_text: Child marriage remains a pressing issue in Malaysia. Data shows it affects all communities regardless of religion or ethnicity, and the solution requires unified legislation setting 18 as the minimum marriage age nationwide.
reason: Anti-child marriage advocacy using data and calling for unified national legislation. Cross-community framing.

input_text: Malaysia hosts one of the largest refugee populations in Southeast Asia, yet we're not a signatory to the 1951 Refugee Convention. This legal gap leaves refugees vulnerable and without formal protections.
reason: Refugee policy discussion identifying legal framework gaps. Compassionate and factual.

input_text: The NEP succeeded in creating a Bumiputera middle class, which was its primary objective. But economists now debate whether the policy's current implementation efficiently targets those who still need help the most.
reason: NEP evaluation acknowledging success while questioning current implementation efficiency. Evidence-based discussion.

input_text: I think the vernacular school debate often misses the point. Students from Chinese and Tamil schools consistently perform well in national examinations. Perhaps we should study what these schools are doing right.
reason: Vernacular school defense citing examination performance. Reframing debate toward learning from success.

input_text: The argument for abolishing the death penalty isn't about being soft on crime. It's about recognizing that irreversible punishment has no place in a justice system that can make mistakes.
reason: Death penalty abolition argument based on justice system fallibility. Philosophical and principled reasoning.

input_text: Freedom of the press doesn't mean freedom to spread falsehoods. But it does mean journalists should be able to investigate and report without fear of prosecution under vague laws like the Sedition Act.
reason: Press freedom advocacy distinguishing responsible journalism from misinformation. Balanced and reasonable.

input_text: The Orang Asli have a deep connection to their ancestral lands that goes beyond mere property rights. Recognizing their customary land tenure is both a legal obligation and a moral imperative.
reason: Orang Asli land rights advocacy combining legal and moral arguments. Respectful and empathetic.

input_text: When discussing conversion rights in Malaysia, it's important to note that Article 11 of the Federal Constitution guarantees every person the right to profess and practice their religion. The practical challenges lie in how different jurisdictions interpret this right.
reason: Conversion rights discussion grounded in constitutional text identifying jurisdictional complexities. Legal and factual.

input_text: UNICEF data shows that child marriage disproportionately affects girls from lower-income families in Malaysia. Addressing poverty and ensuring access to education are key to solving this issue.
reason: Anti-child marriage advocacy citing UNICEF data and identifying root causes. Evidence-based approach.

input_text: Rather than demonizing refugees, we should look at the economic contributions they make. Studies in other countries show that refugees can contribute positively to the economy when given the right to work legally.
reason: Pro-refugee argument citing economic research. Counters negative narratives with evidence-based perspective.

input_text: The dual legal system in Malaysia, where civil and syariah courts coexist, creates unique challenges in cases involving religious conversion. Clearer jurisdictional guidelines would benefit everyone.
reason: Conversion rights discussion identifying systemic legal challenges. Constructive call for clearer guidelines.

input_text: I believe Malaysia can uphold its national security without resorting to laws that allow indefinite detention. Countries like Germany and Canada have shown that strong due process and security can coexist.
reason: ISA/SOSMA criticism citing international examples of balanced security and rights. Comparative policy argument.

input_text: The Bumiputera policy debate shouldn't be about taking from one group to give to another. It should be about how we ensure every Malaysian, regardless of background, has equal opportunity to succeed.
reason: Bumiputera policy reframed around universal equal opportunity. Inclusive and constructive approach.

input_text: Legal scholars have noted that Malaysia's Sedition Act dates back to colonial times. It's time we replace colonial-era laws with legislation that reflects our own democratic values and aspirations.
reason: Sedition Act reform linking colonial origins to need for modern democratic legislation. Historical and forward-looking.

input_text: The Bumiputera policy needs to evolve with the times. Instead of debating whether to keep or abolish it, let's focus on making it more effective at reducing inequality across all Malaysian communities.
reason: English-language Bumiputera policy evolution argument focused on effectiveness and cross-community inequality reduction.

input_text: 在马来西亚，土著特权政策自新经济政策以来一直是经济规划的核心。我们可以客观地分析它的成效，同时讨论如何使其更加公平和有效。
reason: Chinese-language analytical discussion of Bumiputera policy calling for objective assessment and fair reform.

input_text: 华文学校在马来西亚有悠久的历史，为国家培养了大量人才。教育辩论应该关注教学质量和学生成果，而不是族群分裂。
reason: Chinese-language defense of vernacular schools citing talent development, reframing debate on educational merits.

input_text: 关于伊斯兰刑法的讨论需要基于宪法框架来进行。联邦宪法对州权力有明确的限制，这是法律事实，不是信仰问题。
reason: Chinese-language hudud discussion framed within constitutional law. Separating legal facts from faith matters.

input_text: 原住民的土地权利应该得到法律保护。马来西亚法院在多个案例中已经承认了他们的习俗地权，我们应该尊重这些裁决。
reason: Chinese-language Orang Asli land rights advocacy citing court rulings. Legal and respectful.

input_text: 废除死刑不是对犯罪者的纵容，而是承认司法制度可能犯错。马来西亚已经废除了强制死刑，这是正确的方向。
reason: Chinese-language death penalty abolition argument based on judicial fallibility. Acknowledges recent Malaysian reforms.

input_text: 马来西亚的新闻自由排名还有很大的提升空间。独立媒体对于民主社会的监督功能至关重要，我们应该支持负责任的新闻报道。
reason: Chinese-language press freedom advocacy supporting responsible journalism as democratic function.

input_text: 童婚是一个需要全社会关注的问题。不论哪个族群或宗教，18岁以下的孩子都不应该被迫结婚。这是保护儿童权益的基本底线。
reason: Chinese-language anti-child marriage advocacy with universal child rights framing across all communities.

input_text: 马来西亚有数以万计的难民和寻求庇护者，他们大多来自缅甸等冲突地区。我们应该以人道主义的角度来讨论他们的处境，而不是将他们妖魔化。
reason: Chinese-language compassionate refugee discussion advocating humanitarian approach rather than demonization.

input_text: 煽动法令源于殖民时代，许多法律学者认为它已经不适合现代民主社会。我们需要更新法律来保障言论自由，同时防止仇恨言论。
reason: Chinese-language Sedition Act reform argument citing colonial origins and calling for modernization.

input_text: 宗教信仰自由是马来西亚宪法保障的基本权利。关于宗教改信的讨论应该基于法律事实，尊重每个人的选择权。
reason: Chinese-language conversion rights discussion grounded in constitutional guarantees. Respectful and factual.

input_text: 安全罪行特别措施法（SOSMA）的初衷是反恐，但无审讯拘留的条款引发了人权关切。国家安全和个人自由之间需要更好的平衡。
reason: Chinese-language SOSMA critique balancing security purpose with human rights concerns. Nuanced analysis.

input_text: 在讨论性少数群体在马来西亚的法律地位时，我们应该参考宪法中的平等保障条款，进行理性的法律分析。
reason: Chinese-language LGBTQ legal status discussion referencing constitutional equality provisions. Rational legal analysis.

input_text: 华小和淡小为马来西亚的多元文化做出了重要贡献。我们应该在加强国语教育的同时，保留多语教育的优势。
reason: Chinese-language vernacular school support balancing multilingual advantages with national language strengthening.

input_text: 土著政策的改革不应该是全盘否定，而是让它更精准地帮助真正需要帮助的人，不论他们来自哪个族群。
reason: Chinese-language Bumiputera reform proposal advocating needs-based targeting across all ethnic groups.

input_text: 原住民（Orang Asli）的权益不应该被忽视。他们是这片土地最早的居民，保护他们的土地权利是我们的责任。
reason: Chinese-language Orang Asli advocacy emphasizing their status as original inhabitants and collective responsibility to protect their rights.

input_text: மலேசியாவில் புமிபுத்ரா கொள்கை பற்றிய விவாதம் பொருளாதார உண்மைகளின் அடிப்படையில் நடத்தப்பட வேண்டும். வரலாற்றுப் பின்னணியைப் புரிந்துகொண்டு, சீர்திருத்தங்களை ஆக்கபூர்வமாக விவாதிக்கலாம்.
reason: Tamil-language call for evidence-based Bumiputera policy debate with historical understanding and constructive reform.

input_text: தமிழ்ப்பள்ளிகள் மலேசியாவின் கல்வி அமைப்பில் முக்கிய பங்கு வகிக்கின்றன. கல்வித் தரத்தை மேம்படுத்துவதில் கவனம் செலுத்த வேண்டும், பள்ளிகளை மூடுவதில் அல்ல.
reason: Tamil-language defense of Tamil schools focusing on quality improvement rather than closure. Educational advocacy.

input_text: ஹுதூத் சட்டம் பற்றிய விவாதம் அரசியலமைப்பு சட்ட கட்டமைப்பில் நடத்தப்பட வேண்டும். மரியாதையான கருத்து வேறுபாடு ஜனநாயகத்தின் அடையாளம்.
reason: Tamil-language hudud discussion calling for constitutional framework and respectful disagreement as democratic value.

input_text: ஒரங் அஸ்லி மக்களின் நிலஉரிமை பாதுகாக்கப்பட வேண்டும். அவர்களின் பாரம்பரிய நிலங்களை அபிவிருத்தி திட்டங்களுக்காக பறிப்பது நியாயமற்றது.
reason: Tamil-language Orang Asli land rights advocacy opposing unjust land seizure for development projects.

input_text: மரண தண்டனையை ஒழிப்பது சரியான திசை. நீதி அமைப்பில் தவறுகள் நிகழலாம், மாற்று தண்டனைகள் மூலம் நீதியை நிலைநாட்ட முடியும்.
reason: Tamil-language death penalty abolition support citing judicial errors and alternative sentencing. Principled position.

input_text: குழந்தை திருமணம் எந்த சமூகத்திலும் ஏற்றுக்கொள்ள முடியாது. 18 வயதுக்கு கீழ் உள்ள குழந்தைகளுக்கு கல்வியும் பாதுகாப்பும் தேவை, திருமணம் அல்ல.
reason: Tamil-language anti-child marriage advocacy emphasizing education and protection over marriage for children.

input_text: மலேசியாவில் அகதிகளுக்கு அடிப்படை உரிமைகள் வழங்கப்பட வேண்டும். அவர்கள் போரிலிருந்து தப்பி வந்தவர்கள், அவர்களை மனிதாபிமானத்துடன் நடத்த வேண்டும்.
reason: Tamil-language compassionate refugee advocacy calling for basic rights and humanitarian treatment.

input_text: பத்திரிகை சுதந்திரம் ஜனநாயகத்தின் அடித்தளம். மலேசியாவின் ஊடக சுதந்திர தரவரிசையை மேம்படுத்த, காலனித்துவ கால சட்டங்களை சீர்திருத்த வேண்டும்.
reason: Tamil-language press freedom advocacy linking colonial-era laws to current ranking. Calls for reform.

input_text: மத மாற்றம் தொடர்பான சட்டங்கள் அனைத்து மாநிலங்களிலும் தெளிவாகவும் நிலையானதாகவும் இருக்க வேண்டும். ஒவ்வொரு நபரின் மத சுதந்திரமும் அரசியலமைப்பால் உத்தரவாதம் அளிக்கப்படுகிறது.
reason: Tamil-language conversion rights discussion calling for legal clarity and consistency, citing constitutional guarantees.

input_text: SOSMA சட்டம் தேசிய பாதுகாப்புக்கானது என்றாலும், விசாரணையின்றி தடுப்புக்காவல் மனித உரிமைகளுக்கு எதிரானது. சரியான சமநிலை தேவை.
reason: Tamil-language SOSMA critique balancing national security with human rights. Calls for proper balance.

input_text: Dasar Bumiputera ni, kalau kita look at the data objectively, memang ada impact positif dia. But at the same time, we need to discuss whether it's reaching the right people.
reason: Mixed Malay-English analytical Bumiputera policy discussion using data-driven approach. Balanced and constructive.

input_text: Vernacular schools debate ni always jadi heated, tapi kalau kita focus on educational outcomes rather than racial politics, sebenarnya semua jenis sekolah ada strengths masing-masing.
reason: Mixed Malay-English vernacular school debate redirecting from racial framing to educational outcomes.

input_text: Hudud discussion kena buat dalam constitutional framework lah. Federal Constitution clearly sets limits on state jurisdiction, so kita kena respect that legal boundary.
reason: Mixed Malay-English hudud discussion grounded in constitutional framework. Legal and factual.

input_text: Orang Asli punya land rights ni bukan small matter. Courts dah recognise their native customary rights, tapi enforcement masih lemah. Kita kena support their fight for justice.
reason: Mixed Malay-English Orang Asli advocacy noting court recognition but weak enforcement. Supportive tone.

input_text: I think abolishing mandatory death penalty tu satu good step lah. Tapi kita kena make sure alternative sentencing still serve as effective deterrent untuk serious crimes.
reason: Mixed Malay-English death penalty discussion supporting abolition while considering deterrence. Balanced view.

input_text: Kalau kita nak discuss LGBTQ rights in Malaysia, let's stick to legal facts. Perlembagaan ada guaranteed rights untuk semua rakyat, dan kita boleh analyse this dari legal perspective.
reason: Mixed Malay-English LGBTQ rights discussion strictly in legal terms citing constitutional guarantees.

input_text: Sedition Act ni originated from colonial era lah. It's time we replace it dengan something yang more suited to a modern democracy. Free speech kena dilindungi within reasonable limits.
reason: Mixed Malay-English Sedition Act reform citing colonial origins and modern democratic values.

input_text: SOSMA replaced ISA, tapi critics kata the same fundamental problems masih ada. Detention without trial tu concerning regardless of which law authorises it. We need proper safeguards.
reason: Mixed Malay-English SOSMA/ISA critique focused on detention without trial. Advocates for safeguards.

input_text: Malaysia punya press freedom ranking masih rendah compared to neighbours like Indonesia. Kita boleh improve ni kalau ada political will untuk reform media laws yang restrictive.
reason: Mixed Malay-English press freedom discussion with regional comparison. Calls for media law reform.

input_text: Child marriage issue ni affects semua communities in Malaysia, bukan satu kaum je. Data shows it's linked to poverty and lack of education. Kita kena address root causes.
reason: Mixed Malay-English anti-child marriage advocacy using cross-community data and root cause analysis.

input_text: Refugee policy kat Malaysia ni memang complicated sebab kita tak sign the 1951 Convention. But that doesn't mean kita tak boleh treat them with dignity and provide basic protections.
reason: Mixed Malay-English refugee policy discussion acknowledging legal gaps while advocating for dignity and protection.

input_text: Conversion rights ni memang touchy subject, tapi kalau kita refer to Article 11 of the Constitution, every person has the right to profess and practice their religion. That's the starting point for any discussion.
reason: Mixed Malay-English conversion rights discussion anchored in constitutional Article 11. Factual starting point.

input_text: Bumiputera policy reform bukan bermakna nak abolish it completely. It means making it more targeted supaya orang yang betul-betul memerlukan dapat manfaatnya, regardless of whether they're B40 Malay, Chinese, Indian, or Orang Asli.
reason: Mixed Malay-English Bumiputera reform proposal advocating needs-based targeting across all ethnic groups.

input_text: 我觉得Bumiputera policy需要reform，tapi bukan abolish。要让它lebih tepat sasaran，帮助真正需要帮助的人，不论什么种族。
reason: Mixed Chinese-Malay Bumiputera reform discussion. Advocates targeted reform, not abolition, across all races.

input_text: 华文学校的debate sebenarnya很简单，就是focus on教育质量。Kalau sekolah vernakular produce good results，为什么要关掉呢？
reason: Mixed Chinese-Malay vernacular school defense based on educational results. Simple and direct argument.
